items.headline: MCP servers get native CLI auth
items.body: Claude Code 2.1.186 ships `claude mcp login/logout` commands, making MCP authentication a first-class CLI operation. ADO MCP Server connects to Azure DevOps, which has its own auth layer — if that's handled out-of-band today, this is the hook to clean it up. worth checking whether the new flow can replace whatever workaround is in the current implementation.

items.headline: format style beats content for trust boundaries
items.body: research shows LLMs distinguish system vs. user context by format style rather than actual content — mimicking internal thinking blocks can override safety guidelines. LaborQuest feeds user text directly into Claude prompts inside an RPG framing, which is a natural attack surface for this technique. an explicit trust-boundary note in the system prompt is the cheap fix.
